description: Tests whether the model correctly applies learned correction rules to new inputs, and ignores rules when they are not relevant.

cases.input: Get the tire rotated on the truck
cases.notes: Rule should fire — 'tire' is in the keyword list, 'truck' is automotive context.

cases.input: Review the car insurance API integration for the client portal
cases.notes: Rule should NOT fire. 'Car' appears but in a work context (API integration for client). The rule is about personal vehicle tasks, not any mention of 'car'.

rule.rule: Tasks from [boss name] are always priority 4 minimum

cases.input: Dave asked if I could update the team wiki page whenever I get a chance
cases.notes: Rule fires — from Dave, so priority floor is 4 even though 'whenever I get a chance' suggests low priority.

rule.rule: Tasks from [boss name] are always priority 4 minimum

cases.input: Pick up Dave's birthday cake from the bakery
cases.notes: Rule should NOT fire. 'Dave' appears but this is not a task FROM Dave — it's a task about Dave's birthday. Personal domain, normal priority.

cases.input: Schedule time to clean out the closet
cases.notes: Rule fires — personal domain task, scheduling constraint applied.

cases.input: Deploy the hotfix to staging at 8am
cases.notes: Rule should NOT fire — work domain task. The 8am time is fine for work tasks.
